column1: Training Needs Analysis - Staff Questionnaire Responses
Collected: February 2025 | Total Respondents: 147 of 195 invited
Section B: Self-Assessment of Current Skills (1=Very Low  5=Expert)
Section C: Priority Training Areas (Ranked 1=Highest Priority)
Section D: Preferred Learning Formats (Multi-select Y/N)
Section E: Free Text - What one skill would most improve your performance?

column2: I'd love to get a proper AWS Solutions Architect cert. We keep losing bids because clients don't see us as cloud-credible.
Understanding how to use AI tools in my day-to-day coding workflow. I feel like I'm falling behind.
People management skills. I became a team lead overnight with no training whatsoever. I manage 8 people and have no idea how to do performance reviews.
Kubernetes — our projects are moving to container orchestration and I need to be ahead of it.
Python — I came from a .NET background and the team uses Python for everything.
MLOps and how to productionise ML models. I can build models but can't get them into production reliably.
Explainable AI — clients are asking about model transparency and I don't know how to answer them confidently.
Stakeholder communication — I'm a good data scientist but I struggle to explain findings to non-technical execs.
Building a data culture. I manage a team of analysts but the rest of the business doesn't trust or use data in their decisions.
Financial literacy and understanding P&L so I can have better commercial conversations with clients.
Facilitation and running workshops. I need to run discovery workshops with enterprise clients and I'm not confident.
Negotiation skills — renewal conversations with difficult clients are my weak point.
Coaching skills — I want to move beyond advisory HR into proper coaching so I can support the leadership development programme.
Advanced Excel and financial modelling — our current reporting is too manual.
